articles.title: Winter Grass Lowry Nelson
articles.complete_text:
Winter Grass

By Lowry Nelson

Up through the sparse, wind-drifted snow,
Sere and brown upon the winter field
Tufts of summer's grass stand, dry and old—
Lone, stark survivors of the season's yield.

And though the spears are green no more; unto
An autumn fruitfulness no more aspire,
They lend unto the somber field, a hue;
And to the solitary wind, a lyre.
articles.author: Lowry Nelson
articles.notes: Found complete poem on page 75 of the PDF images. It's a short poem with two stanzas describing winter grass.

articles.title: Responsibility of the State in Caring for Its Children Judge Hugo B. Anderson
articles.author: Judge Hugo B. Anderson
articles.notes: The provided PDF images show pages 73-76, but the missing article should be on page 64 according to the table of contents. The images contain content about winter scenes, poetry, and other articles, but do not include the Hugo B. Anderson article about state responsibility for children.
